paper_title: Boundary Slope Fitting and Error Analysis for Lake Bathymetry

methods.description: Boundary slope fitting creates a model of near-shore lake regions using information from nearby regions with similar characteristics. It is used to determine lake bathymetry cost-effectively by adding modeled data points around the lake perimeter, allowing for indirect determination of lake regions without direct depth data.

steps.description: Original bottom data is supplemented with data from the lake edges to determine lake bathymetry.
steps.searchable_summary: supplement bottom data with edge data to model lake bathymetry

steps.description: Use exponential functions to model lake slopes, as polynomials are not flat at the center of the lake.
steps.searchable_summary: apply exponential functions to model lake slopes

methods.description: An error analysis to validate the boundary slope fitting method and the volume estimation of Lib Pond, using Monte Carlo simulations to assess the impact of GPS and modeling errors.

steps.description: Each GPS coordinate was randomly scattered according to an error distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: apply random scatter to GPS coordinates based on error distribution

steps.description: Conduct a Monte Carlo simulation of 500 trials to produce different lake volumes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Monte Carlo simulation for lake volume estimation

steps.description: Add Gaussian distributed random parameters to the exponential function curve fits to test the effect of variance on volume.
steps.searchable_summary: add Gaussian noise to curve fit parameters for variance testing

steps.description: Quantify the effect of boundary slope fitting on volume calculation by comparing boundary slope fitting errors to volume errors.
steps.searchable_summary: compare boundary slope fitting errors to volume errors
